mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in negative earnings revisions and driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, forcing equity analysts to upgrade earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side acceleration and positive economic momentum, triggering short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where INDPRO rises, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 230.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the signal shows no predictive edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 4-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms macroeconomic expansion, reducing recession probability and encouraging institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm synchronized labor and output expansion, triggering short-term momentum flows and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in a walk-forward regression framework.
